verses.text: John the Baptist preaches in the wilderness, 'Repent... the kingdom of heaven is at hand,' the voice crying, Prepare the way. The forerunner. Repentance clears the road for the coming King.
John the Baptist preaches in the wilderness, 'Repent... the kingdom of heaven is at hand,' the voice crying, Prepare the way. The forerunner. Repentance clears the road for the coming King.
John the Baptist preaches in the wilderness, 'Repent... the kingdom of heaven is at hand,' the voice crying, Prepare the way. The forerunner. Repentance clears the road for the coming King.
Clad in camel's hair, eating locusts and honey, John baptizes the crowds confessing their sins. Plain and bold. The herald lives simply and calls for honest confession.
Clad in camel's hair, eating locusts and honey, John baptizes the crowds confessing their sins. Plain and bold. The herald lives simply and calls for honest confession.
Clad in camel's hair, eating locusts and honey, John baptizes the crowds confessing their sins. Plain and bold. The herald lives simply and calls for honest confession.
To the Pharisees and Sadducees he cries, 'O generation of vipers,' bring forth fruit; rely not on Abraham. No empty pedigree. Descent cannot save without real repentance.
To the Pharisees and Sadducees he cries, 'O generation of vipers,' bring forth fruit; rely not on Abraham. No empty pedigree. Descent cannot save without real repentance.
To the Pharisees and Sadducees he cries, 'O generation of vipers,' bring forth fruit; rely not on Abraham. No empty pedigree. Descent cannot save without real repentance.
'The axe is laid unto the root'; One mightier comes who will baptize 'with the Holy Ghost, and with fire.' Greater than John. He points away from himself to Christ.
'The axe is laid unto the root'; One mightier comes who will baptize 'with the Holy Ghost, and with fire.' Greater than John. He points away from himself to Christ.
'The axe is laid unto the root'; One mightier comes who will baptize 'with the Holy Ghost, and with fire.' Greater than John. He points away from himself to Christ.
Jesus comes to be baptized; John forbids, but Jesus says, 'Suffer it... to fulfil all righteousness.' Humble submission. The sinless One identifies with sinners He came to save.
Jesus comes to be baptized; John forbids, but Jesus says, 'Suffer it... to fulfil all righteousness.' Humble submission. The sinless One identifies with sinners He came to save.
Jesus comes to be baptized; John forbids, but Jesus says, 'Suffer it... to fulfil all righteousness.' Humble submission. The sinless One identifies with sinners He came to save.
As Jesus rises, the Spirit descends like a dove, and a voice says, 'This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.' The Trinity revealed. Father, Son, and Spirit are seen together at His baptism.
